article_meta.title: close Video American Accountability Foundation president releases Education Department 'watch list,' explains why Trump

reasoning: The model repeatedly flagged quoted or clearly attributed source language as author bias and missed at least one clear instance in the author's own prose (“so-called book bans”). One label (“Omission of Source Attribution”) was also misapplied.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All four flagged items derive from quoted or attributed statements (Tom Jones, DOE/agency statements), violating the attribution rule.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Every flagged instance is not author-level bias; they are quotes or attributed claims, and one is mischaracterized as lacking attribution.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed clear author-prose bias: the author’s use of “so-called book bans,” a skeptical qualifier indicating slant/word choice.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Labels are moot given attribution errors; additionally, “Omission of Source Attribution” was misapplied since the claim was attributed to DOE.
